question_frequency.rationale: The Pair asks zero didactic questions. It dumps the complete optimal solution with explanation in a single turn, and the user simply confirms. There are no teachable moments surfaced as choices.

question_quality.rationale: No questions or choices are framed at all. The two-option didactic format the project is designed around is entirely absent — no fork between e.g. merge-based O(m+n) vs partition-based O(log min(m,n)), no choice on handling odd/even totals or boundary sentinels.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no scaffolding because there is no progression. The full solution, invariant explanation, and complexity analysis are delivered in one block with no graduated reveal.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is clear and the explanation is well-organized, but it's a monologue rather than a conversation. The user's only contribution is 'Perfect!' — there's no back-and-forth at all.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
